# Extracao: transcription_mp4_12__Reputation

---

Reputation. You have a reputation, a black eye, that you don't know about and you need to find out what it is. Maybe you need to search the internet. What did they see that you need to see? It's a Yelp review, a Google review, something on Facebook. Somebody posted something or said something, even more dangerous, said something about your company and you need to handle it. Look, the best way to handle it is not to remove a bad reputation review. The best way to handle your reputation is to get a good reputation. That means to get lots of people saying good things about you and for your company to start saying good things about your company. Most companies that I work with basically just don't tell the world what they're doing to contribute to the world. They wait until there's a reputation problem and then they're playing catch up every day. In some way, you need to tell the world what you're doing to contribute, to make it different, to make your area, your community, your group better. You need to build and sell your reputation. The old adage, oh, I don't want to blow my own horn. Well, then why have a horn? That's stupid, okay? You need to build your reputation and your brand and you need to make it golden, but you don't need it to be perfect. Let me tell you something. If you're going to make it to the top, you're going to have haters. You'll have people post stuff about you that is not true. So what? Don't worry about it. Get all the people that say great things about you to post that and that'll overwhelm the one hater. Anybody that's successful is going to have haters. Never worry about the haters. Worry about those black eyes that you don't know people are saying. You don't know the reputation that you might have. Ask people. Just ask them straight up. Have you heard something about me or my company or about our product that is keeping you from doing business with us? Have you? Just watch for an answer. The answer will be there before you hear anything. You'll see it show up on that buyer's face. Well, yeah, I did. What did they say? Don't worry about who said it right now. What did they say? And then what you need to do is provide real hard data that says that's not us. Of course, people say bad things. I mean, people are going to say bad things. I'm on the move. I'm taking over the market. Okay, they don't want to lose their market to me. Here's the truth about what me and my company does. And that needs to be solved by you selling the reputation of your company, not just once, but every day in every way. And that's why social media, the Googles, the Facebooks, the LinkedIn, the Twitters are so important for you to be there every day saying, this is who we are. Reputation can kill a deal. Protect yours by telling people what you're doing well.
